title: MCU Catalog Meta-Schema
description: Validates the structure of data/mcu_catalog.json. Each top-level key is an MCU type name (e.g. 'MCU_Timer'). Each value is a catalog entry describing that type's required/optional fields, value types, child blocks, and FMEA constraints. Produced by Phase 0.4; consumed by PI-001 validator and the Module Reverse Engineer.

catalog_entry.title: CatalogEntry
catalog_entry.description: Schema definition for a single MCU type.

type.description: The MCU type name, matching the enclosing key.

required_fields.description: Field names that must be present in every instance of this MCU type (present in 100% of corpus instances).

optional_fields.description: Field names that may appear in some but not all instances of this MCU type (present in <100% of corpus instances).

field_types.description: Maps each field name to its expected Python type string. Lists are resolved to typed variants (list[int], list[float], list[str]) where corpus data permits.

child_blocks.description: Shallow mapping of child block type names to their total occurrence count across all corpus instances of this MCU type. Populated by build_mcu_catalog.py. Full recursive child block schemas are Phase 1 scope (D-19).

^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]*$.description: Total number of times this child block type was observed across all corpus instances of the parent MCU type.

constraints.description: FMEA constraint annotation strings. Descriptive only — enforcement logic belongs in compiler primitives, not the catalog (per review triage decision, 2026-03-19).

field_type_value.description: Expected Python type for a field. 'list[int]', 'list[float]', 'list[str]' are the resolved typed variants. 'list' is the unresolved fallback for empty-only fields.
